{{ og_all_tags }} {{ auto_includes_css }}
Docs

doma

A small Crystal CLI for tagging directories, then jumping to them or running commands across the whole pile.

I kept losing track of where things lived — twelve Crystal repos in one folder, seven shell experiments in another, the security write-ups elsewhere. cd's history wasn't enough; fuzzy finders forgot what mattered the moment I cleared the terminal. So I wrote a cutting board: pin the directories I care about, label them with whatever tags make sense (crystal, work/proj-a, reading-this-week), and let the tool do the recall. 

# put a directory on the board
$ doma add ~/Projects/doma -t crystal -t cli

# jump to one (interactive picker if there's more than one match)
$ doma cd crystal

# operate on the whole pile
$ doma list -t crystal --paths | xargs -I{} sh -c 'cd {} && shards build'

# bookmark the current dir for the week (auto-expires after 7 days)
$ doma mark spike
Read the docs View on GitHub

It's a single static binary, written in Crystal, with a built-in TUI picker so you don't need fzf. Tags can be permanent or expire on a TTL. There's a shell wrapper (doma setup install) that makes doma cd actually change your working directory, and a Claude Code skill so an agent can treat your tagged paths as a first-class input list.

Doma (도마) is the Korean word for cutting board — the workbench where ingredients get gathered, grouped, and chopped before going into the pan. The metaphor stuck.

MIT · written in Crystal github.com/hahwul/doma
